asset_name: CJC-1295 First-in-Human GH and IGF-1 Pharmacology

publication.title: Prolonged stimulation of growth hormone and insulin-like growth factor I secretion by CJC-1295 in healthy adults

study_design: Two randomised, placebo-controlled, double-blind ascending-dose trials lasting 28 and 49 days.
population_or_model: Healthy adults aged 21–61 years at two investigational sites.
intervention_or_exposure: Subcutaneous single ascending doses followed by weekly or biweekly repeated-dose cohorts.
outcomes: Single-dose CJC-1295 produced dose-dependent mean GH increases of approximately 2- to 10-fold for at least 6 days.
Mean IGF-1 increased approximately 1.5- to 3-fold for 9–11 days.
After multiple doses, mean IGF-1 remained above baseline for up to 28 days.
No serious adverse reactions were reported in these small short-duration trials.
limitations: Healthy volunteer pharmacology, not therapeutic efficacy.
The studied DAC compound is not modified GRF (1-29).
permitted_claims: CJC-1295 with DAC produced sustained GH and IGF-1 elevation in early healthy-adult trials.
The compound had a multi-day estimated half-life in the study.
prohibited_claims: Transfer of results to non-DAC products or blends.
